task_completion.description: The agent initializes an 'Open Curve' markup object to trace the vessel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Markups' module is visible, or the agent clicks the 'Create Open Curve' icon in the toolbar. A new curve object appears in the Data list or Subject Hierarchy.

task_completion.description: The agent places multiple control points (at least 8) along the center of the aorta in the axial view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the axial slice view (transverse cross-section), control points are visible centered within the bright, circular aorta. The agent scrolls through slices placing points from superior to inferior.

task_completion.description: The agent navigates to the 'Curved Planar Reformat' module and selects the correct inputs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Curved Planar Reformat' module panel is displayed. The 'Input Curve' is set to the created markup, and 'Input Volume' is set to the CT scan.

task_completion.description: The agent generates the CPR, resulting in a view of the straightened aorta.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A slice view in the layout changes to show the 'straightened' aorta: a long, bright, tubular structure running vertically or horizontally across the view, distinct from the original axial/sagittal/coronal views.

task_completion.description: The agent saves the CPR image and the centerline curve to the specified paths.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save' dialog, 'Export to file' context menu, or file browser interaction is visible. The filenames 'aorta_cpr.png' and 'aorta_centerline.json' are entered or visible in the target directory '~/Documents/SlicerData/Exports/'.

integrity.description: The agent uses the 3D Slicer GUI modules (Markups, Curved Planar Reformat) rather than scripting the entire process.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with module panels, dropdowns, and the 3D/slice views, not just a Python console or terminal window.

integrity.description: The agent manually identifies the aorta rather than importing a pre-made curve.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen clicking on the axial slices to place points, rather than loading a .json file immediately at the start.

integrity.description: The output files are generated via the application's export features.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent uses Slicer's save/export dialogs or context menus, not a terminal command like 'cp' or 'mv' to rename existing files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The abdominal aorta is a large artery located anterior to the spine. In the provided contrast-enhanced CT, it appears as a bright white circle in axial slices. The Curved Planar Reformation (CPR) result should transform this circular cross-section into a long, straight, bright tube.
